pi_items.verified_value: Confirmed as target directory where .vital files are copied during task setup

pi_items.verified_value: All three files exist at benchmarks/cua_world/environments/vital_recorder_windows_env/data/ with confirmed content

pi_items.verified_value: All four event names found in the decompressed binary content of each .vital file. Exact match in case 0001: 'Case started', 'Surgery started', 'Surgery finished', 'Case finished'; same in cases 0002 and 0003
pi_items.source: Binary file inspection by decompressing gzip-compressed .vital files and searching for event name strings
4 events per case × 3 cases = 12 total events. Verified by finding all 4 events in each of the 3 case files.

pi_items.verified_value: VitalDB (Vital Signs DataBase) is a real, open-access dataset containing 6,388 surgical cases with high-resolution vital signs monitoring data. Available on PhysioNet (https://physionet.org/content/vitaldb/1.0.0/) under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 license. Cases 0001, 0002, 0003 are documented on PhysioNet with verified SHA256 checksums.
pi_items.source: Web search verification; PhysioNet official documentation; case files found in official VitalDB repository

pi_items.verified_value: Data originates from non-cardiac surgeries (general, thoracic, urologic, gynaecologic) at Seoul National University Hospital, South Korea. Recorded using Vital Recorder v1.7.4 from anesthesia monitoring devices (Solar 8000, BIS, Primus, SNUADC, Orchestra). Cases involve synchronized monitoring of 196+ intraoperative parameters.
pi_items.source: VitalDB documentation on PhysioNet; binary file inspection showing real device monitoring data (device names and parameters embedded in vital files)

pi_items.metadata_value: Case started=pre-operative, Surgery started=start of intraoperative, Surgery finished=end of intraoperative, Case finished=end of post-operative
pi_items.verified_value: Standard perioperative phases: Pre-operative (before OR transfer), Intraoperative (from OR entry to PACU transfer), Post-operative (from PACU to recovery completion). The mapping stated in task may reflect institutional protocol where 'Case started' represents pre-anesthesia phase before incision.
pi_items.source: Medical literature search on perioperative period definitions; VitalDB documentation references event markers for surgery start/end and anesthesia start/end times

pi_items.verified_value: File naming convention is reasonable and follows task specification. CSV files are expected outputs to be created by the agent during task execution.
pi_items.source: Task specification and verifier code analysis

pi_items.verified_value: Database file path and naming specified in task.json and verifier code. Content requirements: ≥400 bytes, contains case IDs (0001, 0002, 0003), event names, and numeric time data.
pi_items.source: Task metadata and verifier.py code analysis
Verifier code defines PASS_THRESHOLD = 60 and scoring rubric allocates 100 points total across 5 criteria (20 points each)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses three real intraoperative vital signs recordings from the VitalDB open dataset publicly available on PhysioNet. Cases 0001, 0002, and 0003 are documented cases from Seoul National University Hospital containing synchronized monitoring of 196+ parameters from actual surgical procedures. Each case file contains exactly four standard event markers: 'Case started', 'Surgery started', 'Surgery finished', and 'Case finished', yielding 12 total events across all three cases. The data is not synthetic—it comes from real surgical patients (non-cardiac procedures) recorded with Vital Recorder v1.7.4 from clinical monitoring devices (Solar 8000, BIS, Primus, SNUADC, Orchestra). The VitalDB dataset is licensed under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 and is freely accessible for research and development purposes.
